acceptance.proof: Encoding uses CRLF after all three lines; the pinned public example is exactly 65 bytes; parsing admits only complete CRLF delimiters and reports the exact first failing offset.
Focused tests and fixtures accept CRLF and reject LF-only, mixed endings, missing CR, extra CR or LF, truncated, oversized, and noncanonical decimal payloads at exact first offsets.
Provenance distinguishes RST code-block indentation from packet bytes, binds the 65-byte logged count, records the CRLF inference, and retains rejected LSLC-002C as historical evidence.
No sockets, runtime discovery, endpoint semantics, clocks, interoperability, device effects, dependencies, features, unsafe, or Manifold authority are added.

commit_policy: Correct LSLC-002C additively; preserve its commits, lifecycle, receipt, push plan, and primary rejection history.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the additive LSLC-002D CRLF correction and rejected 002C evidence.
Correct public query byte-shape documentation and evidence limits.
Review public/private and repo routing boundaries.
Review artifact roles, additive failure evidence, exact rejection behavior, closed activation, and authority.

non_scope: Query evaluation, response shape, endpoint semantics, sockets, multicast, discovery runtime, retries, clocks, currentness, providers, or interoperability.
Installed liblsl or rLSL implementation source, external oracle execution, dependencies, features, unsafe, FFI, devices, or hidden activation.
Manifold admission, registry, subscription, route, lease, session, topology, revision, replay, authorization, audit, transport, product, command, or provider authority.
objective: Additively correct LSLC-002C to the exact CRLF-terminated 65-byte public-example candidate and preserve the rejected LF-only unit as failure evidence.
outputs: CRLF-only canonical encoder and borrowed parser with exact line-ending failure offsets.
